finding: Team-start evidence preserved one autonomous selection, but no shared artifact chained that decision to implementation commits, verification evidence, and peer-trigger attempts while making retroactive mutation detectable.

observed: M-015 created a fail-closed, commit-bound team-start evidence packet with a deterministic selection digest.
The new ledger contract defines ordered entries that bind team-start digest, queue item, implementation commits, verification references, peer-trigger attempts, and the prior entry digest.
The validator rejects sequence drift, malformed identifiers, broken predecessor links, digest mismatch, invalid peer-trigger records, and append attempts against an already invalid ledger.
Six deterministic and adversarial tests are committed.
inferred: A required hash-linked ledger makes silent retroactive alteration detectable when validators re-evaluate the complete chain.
proposed: Create the first production ledger only when a genuine accepted team-start packet is available; do not synthesize a startup digest.
Require each team completion path to append one entry after implementation and verification, then preserve the resulting ledger as immutable run evidence.
superseded: Treating separate queue updates, evidence files, commits, and trigger logs as a sufficient chronological chain without explicit linkage.
Allowing later run summaries to describe prior execution without binding to the prior record digest.
unresolved: No production ledger entry was created because this automation run did not expose a persisted accepted M-016 team-start packet or its selection digest.
The committed Node tests have not been observed running in CI or a clean checkout.
Hash chaining detects mutation but does not provide an external timestamp, signature, or independent witness.

privacy_boundary: Only public repository operations, abstract queue identifiers, commit identities, synthetic fixtures, and peer-team identifiers were used. Raw private chat, health and pet-health material, credentials, personal identifiers, and unreviewed uploads were excluded.
falsification_route: Mutate any accepted historical entry, alter its predecessor digest, reorder entries, duplicate or abbreviate commit SHAs, or append to an invalid ledger; validation must fail with a machine-readable error.
